title: Reported road casualties in Great Britain, provisional estimates: year ending June 2025
body: " Our statistical practice is regulated by the Office for Statistics Regulation (OSR). OSR sets the standards of trustworthiness, quality and value in the Code of Practice for Statistics that all producers of official statistics should adhere to. You are welcome to contact us directly by emailing us with any comments about how we meet these standards.   Reported road casualties for the year ending June 2025 showed a continuation of the trends seen over the last decade.  In reported road collisions in Great Britain in the year ending June 2025 there were an estimated:    1,579 fatalities, a decline of 3% compared to the year ending June 2024   29,896 killed or seriously injured (KSI) casualties, little change compared to the year ending June 2024   127,161 casualties of all severities, a decline of 3% compared to the year ending June 2024    Alongside the main publication  we are publishing statistics on self reported driver behaviour from the Crime Survey for England and Wales for the year ending March 2025, as well as an update to our report on linking police and health data to reflect developments in the linkage methodology.   Alongside the publication of annual road casualty statistics for 2024 we published a user feedback request on proposed changes to road casualty statistics.  We have not received any feedback on these changes and therefore were are planning to go ahead with the changes listed, starting with the publication of the final annual casualty statistics for 2025 schedule for publication in September 2026.  We continue to welcome feedback on the planned changes, or any other aspect of road casualty statistics.   The next reported road casualty statistics, provisional annual estimates for the whole of 2025, are scheduled for publication in May 2026.  Contact us  Road safety statistics     Email roadacc.stats@dft.gov.uk         To hear more about DfT statistical publications as they are released, follow us on X at DfTstats.  "
